baseline_note: Every platform benchmark expects Domain protocol + Data implementation separation. No URLSession, FoundationModels, or other platform framework imports in the Domain layer.

evals.prompt: Set up a URLSession API client with token authentication and automatic refresh for my iOS app
evals.expected_output: APIClientProtocol in Domain, URLSessionAPIClient in Data, KeychainTokenProvider actor
expectations: Defines APIClientProtocol in the Domain layer with no URLSession imports
Implements URLSessionAPIClient in the Data layer
Implements token refresh that retries the original request on 401

evals.prompt: Set up a typed navigation router with deep linking using a custom URL scheme for my iOS app
evals.expected_output: AppRouter (@Observable @MainActor), typed AppRoute enum, DeepLinkResolver, .onOpenURL wiring
expectations: Defines a typed AppRoute enum with Hashable conformance
Implements a DeepLinkResolver that maps URLs to typed AppRoute destinations
Wires .onOpenURL or .onContinueUserActivity at the app entry point

evals.prompt: Integrate Apple Foundation Models for on-device text summarization with a fallback when the feature is unavailable
evals.expected_output: Domain protocol with no FoundationModels imports, FoundationModels-backed implementation with runtime availability check, deterministic fallback
expectations: Domain protocol has no FoundationModels imports
Implementation checks runtime availability before calling FoundationModels APIs
Provides a deterministic fallback path when Foundation Models is unavailable
FoundationModels imports are confined to the Data layer

evals.prompt: Create a privacy manifest for an iOS app that uses UserDefaults, location, and crash reporting
evals.expected_output: PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy with NSPrivacyAccessedAPITypes for UserDefaults and NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypes for location and crash data
expectations: Generates a valid PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy XML or plist structure
Declares NSUserDefaults under NSPrivacyAccessedAPITypes with a valid reason code
Includes location data type under NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypes
